title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: John Benoit, a 63-year-old active-duty Everett firefighter, died from occupational cancer after over 30 years of service.
Benoit, an Air Force veteran, was appointed to the Everett Fire Department in 1996, fulfilling a family legacy.
A wake will be held at JF Ward Funeral Home; his funeral Mass is at Our Lady of Grace Church in Chelsea.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dorchester women Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz charged in a multi-state human trafficking scheme.
Recci allegedly forced a homeless victim into sex work, using fear and confiscating personal belongings.
Investigators linked suspects to crimes using social media and GPS, finding firearms and drugs at arrest site.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, also known as Rohan Rhys, drowned in South Watuppa Pond on July 12.
Police divers recovered Major's body after a two-hour search; no foul play is suspected.
A GoFundMe was created by his mother to cover funeral expenses and honor his memory.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke performance at Gaslight in downtown Nantucket.
The karaoke night included co-stars Chloë Sevigny, Regina Hall, Gemma Chan, and D’Arcy Carden singing ABBA’s “Dancing Queen.”
Local guide Bill Jamieson joined them on stage; Garner praised him and mentioned his Ghosts of Nantucket tour.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Canadian wildfire smoke is causing yellow skies and a smoky haze over the Boston region.
The National Weather Service states the smoke has limited expected heat due to combined cloud cover.
Meteorologists predict the smoky haze and yellow skies will persist through Wednesday, affecting temperatures.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A special traffic cone from Glasgow, signed by Stuart Patrick, arrived in Boston to symbolize a new city partnership.
The cone, promoting mental health fundraising, will be showcased around Boston by civic and tourism partners.
Boston and Glasgow announced efforts to become sister cities, building connections begun during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three more MBTA employees were charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspections at Cabot Yard.
Seven former MBTA employees, including supervisors and inspectors, pleaded not guilty to new federal conspiracy charges.
Prosecutors allege the employees submitted false inspection reports and worked on personal vehicles during work hours.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won the last World Cup game held in Foxborough, concluding Boston's host role in the tournament.
Boston was top-ranked among U.S. World Cup venues, enhancing pride and spurring local interest in hosting major events.
Boston's previous Olympic bid required costly infrastructure projects, with estimates originally at $4.7 billion, unlike the World Cup's $100 million expenses.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, Bill Belichick's daughter-in-law, is wanted for arrest in North Carolina for missing a court date.
She was cited for driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County.
Bill Belichick is head coach at UNC; UNC went 4-8 last season under his leadership.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and Aidan Kearney seek dismissal of a defamation suit filed by witnesses in Read's murder case.
Witnesses claim Read and Kearney's accusations have turned them into 'prisoners' in their communities.
Read and Kearney argue their actions are protected under Massachusetts's anti-SLAPP law aimed at protecting free speech.
